protocol_definition.name: Voting
protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents answer independently, then cast private ballots; plurality selects the output.
protocol_definition.visibility: Answers are private before voting; voters see all anonymized proposals during voting.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after ballot aggregation; deterministic lowest-index tie break.

intermediate_messages.content:
1. System overview
The service enables users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files, validates size and type, stores originals with metadata, extracts text, segments it into searchable sections, builds an index, and supports search across documents. Processing can be asynchronous, with retry on failures. Duplicate uploads of the same file (by content hash) are deduplicated. The design emphasizes modularity, easy extension for new formats, maintainability, and a small developer footprint.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / Frontend API | Accepts uploads, searches, and status queries; authenticates and routes to services. | HTTP requests (upload, search, status). | HTTP responses with results/ statuses. |
| Upload Validator | Verifies file type, size, and computes content hash for deduplication. | File, metadata. | Validation result, content hash, allowed/denied flag. |
| Storage Service | Persists original file and metadata; serves for retrieval. | File bytes, metadata. | Storage location reference (ID/URI). |
| Metadata Store | Stores document metadata (owner, upload time, hash, format, status). | Metadata records. | Metadata IDs, status updates. |
| Text Extraction Engine | Extracts raw text from supported formats. | Original file reference. | Extracted text payload. |
| Document Divider / Segmenter | Splits extracted text into searchable sections (e.g., by headings, paragraphs). | Extracted text. | List of text segments with metadata (offsets, tags). |
| Indexer / Search Engine | Builds and maintains a searchable index from segments; supports queries. | Text segments. | Inverted index entries; searchable index state. |
| Processing Orchestrator (Worker / Queue) | Orchestrates asynchronous processing steps; handles retries on failure; tracks progress. | Upload event or message with document reference. | Task progress/status updates; re-queue on retryable failures. |
| Status & Progress Reporter | Exposes processing status per file/document; reports completed/failed/in-progress. | Metadata/processing state. | Status payloads for API clients. |
| Deduplication & Registry | Detects repeated uploads (by content hash) and reuses existing records; avoids duplicates. | Content hash, existing records. | Reference to existing document or new document record. |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload request arrives via API Gateway.
- Validation:
  - Check MIME type and extension against supported list (PDF, TXT, MD/Markdown).
  - Check file size against configured maximum.
  - Compute content hash (e.g., SHA-256) to enable deduplication.
- Deduplication:
  - If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document reference and status; do not create new storage or index entries.
  - If new, create a document record with status “queued” and store the original file and basic metadata.
- Persistence:
  - Persist original file in file storage (e.g., object store) and save metadata in the metadata store.
- Asynchronous processing (orchestrated by a queue):
  - Enqueue a processing job for the new document.
  - Step 1: Text Extraction Engine extracts text from the file.
  - Step 2: Divider splits text into sections/segments.
  - Step 3: Indexer builds/upserts the index from segments.
  - Step 4: Update document status to “completed” with index reference.
- Status reporting:
  - Clients can poll or subscribe to status; statuses include: queued, extracting, dividing, indexing, completed, failed, retrying.
- Search:
  - Search requests query the inverted index; results map back to document segments and provide document metadata and snippet highlights.

4. REST API design

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /upload | Upload a file for processing | multipart/form-data: file, optional metadata (owner, tags) | { document_id, status: "queued" |, hash } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata and status | - | { id, owner, format, hash, status, created_at, updated_at, index_id } |
| POST | /search | Search across documents | { query, filters? (document_id, date range, tags) } | { results: [ { document_id, snippet, score, highlights, metadata } ] } |
| GET | /documents/{id}/status | Get processing status/details | - | { id, status, progress, steps: [{ name, status, detail }] } |
| GET | /health | System health | - | { status: "ok" } |

Notes:
- The /upload endpoint returns early with a document_id; the client can poll /documents/{id}/status or subscribe to progress.
- Search results include document_id, snippet, score, and highlights mapped to segments.

5. Data model

- Document
  - id (UUID)
  - owner_id (optional)
  - original_filename
  - hash (SHA-256)
  - format (PDF/TXT/MD)
  - size_bytes
  - created_at
  - updated_at
  - status (queued, extracting, dividing, indexing, completed, failed)
  - index_id (reference to index state)
  - metadata (JSON blob for arbitrary fields)

- Segment
  - id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - segment_index
  - text
  - start_offset, end_offset (character offsets)
  - metadata (section_heading, tags)

- IndexEntry (per term)
  - term
  - document_id
  - segment_id
  - score
  - metadata ( tf-idf-ish stats, positions )

- OriginalFileStorage
  - records mapping document_id → storage_uri, storage_bucket, version, checksum

- ProcessingJob
  - id (UUID)
  - document_id
  - step (extraction/division/indexing)
  - status (queued/in-progress/succeeded/failed/retrying)
  - tries
  - last_updated

6. Suggested project structure

- cmd/
  - api_server/ (HTTP API endpoints)
- internal/
  - api/
  - validation/
  - storage/
  - metadata/
  - text_extractor/
  - segmenter/
  - index/
  - queue/
  - processor/
  - reporting/
- pkg/
  - models/
  - errors/
- config/
  - default.yaml
- test/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- docs/
  - architecture.md

Code organization principles:
- Clear separation between API layer, domain logic, and infrastructure.
- Stateless HTTP API; use a small, durable storage for metadata and a separate object store for files.
- Dependency injection to swap out format handlers and storage backends.
- Single-responsibility components with well-defined interfaces.

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size) return 400 with actionable error codes.
- Transient failures (network/storage hiccups, temporary extractor errors) are retryable:
  - Processing jobs are placed in a durable queue with exponential backoff.
  - If a step fails with a retryable error, mark the job as retrying and re-queue; cap total retries (e.g., 5) with backoff.
- Non-retryable failures (corrupt file, unsupported content) mark document status as failed with reason.
- Duplicate prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload.
  - Before creating a new document record, check if a document with the same hash exists.
  - If exists and status is completed or indexing done, return reference to existing document; if exists but processing incomplete, return current status and avoid duplicate storage.
- Idempotency:
  - idempotent /upload using the same hash can be supported by clients; server-side dedupe guarantees no duplicates.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests:
  - Validation module: reject invalid types, large sizes; accept valid ones.
  - Hashing and deduplication: identical files map to same document id.
  - Text extraction mocks for each format; ensure correct payload handling.
  - Segmenter: correct segmentation rules across sample texts.
  - Indexer: verify inverted index entries and retrieval results.
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end for a small PDF/TXT/MD sample: upload -> status progression -> search results.
  - Retry flow: simulate transient failure in a step and ensure retry occurs and eventual success.
  - Duplicate upload path: same file uploaded twice yields single document and proper references.
- Performance/load tests:
  - Concurrent uploads and searches; verify queue backpressure and acceptable latency.
- Security tests:
  - Validate access control on endpoints; ensure no leakage of raw files without authorization.

9. Design tradeoff

- Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and resilience but adds eventual consistency; search results may depend on the index being built; mitigate with status indicators and optional synchronous previews if needed.
- Deduplication by content hash reduces storage and confusion but requires careful handling of hash collisions (negligible risk with SHA-256, but plan for collision-safe paths).
- Support for new formats is designed via a plug-in style architecture for text extraction and segmentation; initial formats (PDF, TXT, MD) implemented with minimal dependencies, with an extensible registry for future formats.
- Indexing strategy focuses on a simple inverted index with per-document segments; this balances search quality and simplicity for a small team, with room to upgrade to full-text search engines if scale grows.

Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway | Accepts upload, search, status; routing | HTTP requests | Responses, queue events |
| Upload Validator | Type/size validation; compute hash | File, metadata | Validation result, hash |
| Storage Service | Persist originals | File bytes, metadata | Storage reference |
| Metadata Store | Document metadata | Metadata records | Document IDs, status updates |
| Text Extraction Engine | Extract text per format | Original file | Extracted text |
| Document Divider | Segment text into sections | Extracted text | Segments |
| Indexer | Build/searchable index | Segments | Inverted index entries |
| Processing Orchestrator | Manage async pipeline; retries | Processing jobs | Status updates |
| Status Reporter | Provide status to clients | Document state | Status payload |
| Deduplication Registry | Detect duplicates | Hash, existing records | Existing or new document reference |

API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /upload | Upload a file for processing | multipart: file, owner_id, tags | { document_id, status } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata and status | - | { id, owner, format, hash, status, index_id } |
| POST | /search | Search across documents | { query, filters } | { results: [ { document_id, snippet, score, highlights } ] } |
| GET | /documents/{id}/status | Get processing status | - | { id, status, progress_steps } |
| GET | /health | System health | - | { status: "ok" } |

This design yields a maintainable, extensible backend architecture for a document-processing and search service suitable for a small team, with clear data flow, robust error handling, and a path to supporting new formats and larger-scale needs.

1. System overview
The service is a backend system for processing and searching document content. Users upload PDF, TXT, or Markdown files. Each upload triggers validation, storage of the original file and metadata, text extraction, segmentation into searchable chunks, indexing, and a searchable interface. Processing can be asynchronous; steps may fail and be retried. Duplicate uploads of the same file are deduplicated by content hash. The architecture emphasizes ease of extension for new formats, maintainability for a small team, and clear data flow from upload to search.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / Frontend Interface | Authenticate, validate request, route to upload endpoint; provide status and search endpoints | HTTP requests from clients | Responses with status, metadata, search results |
| File Validator & Metadata Extractor | Validate file type/size; extract basic metadata (name, size, mime, hash) | Uploaded file | Validation result, file metadata, content hash |
| Storage Service | Persist original file and metadata; provide durable retrieval | File binary + metadata | Stored file references (IDs/paths) |
| Text Extraction Engine | Convert file to plain text for indexing (PDF, TXT, MD) | Stored file | Extracted plain text |
| Document Segmentation Service | Split text into searchable chunks with consistent metadata (offsets, section IDs) | Plain text | List of text chunks with chunk-level metadata |
| Indexing Service | Build and update a searchable index (inverted index, embeddings, or hybrid) | Chunks + metadata | Index entries; status |
| Search Service | Execute user queries against index; support ranking and pagination | Query string + filters | Ranked list of document/chunk results with snippets |
| Processing Orchestrator / Job Queue | Orchestrate asynchronous steps, retries, and dependency sequencing | Upload events, status signals | Updated processing state, job completion events |
| Status & Notification Manager | Report processing status per uploaded file; expose status to API | Internal status updates | User-facing status payloads |
| Deduplication & Repository Layer | Detect duplicates by content hash; prevent unnecessary records | File hash, existing records | Reference to existing record or new record |
| Configuration & Extension Layer | Centralize formats, pipelines, and retry policies | Config data | Applied configuration for components |
| Logging & Observability | Collect logs, metrics, traces for diagnostics | System events | Telemetry data for dashboards |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- User uploads file (PDF/TXT/MD) via API.
- Validation: check MIME/type, size limit; compute content hash; look up existing document by hash.
- If duplicate: return existing document reference and status; skip reprocessing.
- Persist: store original file and metadata in durable storage; create a document record with status “queued” or “processing” as appropriate.
- Asynchronous pipeline (or synchronous fallback):
  - Text Extraction: extract plain text from the stored file (PDF parsing, plaintext read, Markdown rendering).
  - Segmentation: divide text into chunks with metadata (section id, offset, length) suitable for search.
  - Indexing: index chunks (inverted index; optional semantic embeddings if desired) and attach document-level metadata.
  - Status update: mark document as “ready” when indexing finishes; if failure occurs, mark as “failed” and emit retry policy.
- API can serve search queries against the index, returning ranked results with snippet previews.
- Status endpoint reports current state (queued, processing, ready, failed, retried count, last error).

4. REST API design

- Upload endpoint (POST /documents)
  - Purpose: submit a file for processing
  - Main Request Data: multipart/form-data with file; optional metadata (tags, author)
  - Main Response Data: document_id, status (queued/processing/ready/failed), hash, size, created_at

- Status endpoint (GET /documents/{id})
  - Purpose: query current processing status and metadata
  - Main Request Data: document_id path parameter
  - Main Response Data: status, progress percentage, last_error (if any), metadata, references to stored file

- Search endpoint (GET /search)
  - Purpose: search across processed documents
  - Main Request Data: query string; optional filters (document_id, tag, date); pagination (page, per_page)
  - Main Response Data: results: array of {document_id, score, snippet, metadata}; total

- Health endpoint (GET /health)
  - Purpose: service health and readiness checks

- Internal endpoints (optional, for admin/ops)
  - Retry/ Retry policy management
  - Reindex all documents

5. Data model

- Document
  - document_id: string (UUID)
  - hash: string (content hash, e.g., SHA-256)
  - filename: string
  - mime_type: string
  - size: number
  - created_at: timestamp
  - status: enum {queued, processing, ready, failed}
  - last_error: string (nullable)
  - tags: list<string>
  - metadata: JSON (arbitrary metadata)

- FileStorage
  - file_id: string
  - document_id: string (FK)
  - path_or_uri: string
  - stored_at: timestamp

- TextExtraction
  - extraction_id: string
  - document_id: string (FK)
  - texto: string (full extracted text or large blob)
  - extracted_at: timestamp

- Chunk (Segmented text)
  - chunk_id: string
  - document_id: string (FK)
  - chunk_index: int
  - content: string
  - start_offset: int
  - end_offset: int
  - created_at: timestamp

- IndexEntry
  - index_id: string
  - document_id: string (FK)
  - chunk_id: string (FK)
  - term_or_vector: string (token or embedding)
  - frequency: int
  - position: int (optional)

- SearchResult
  - document_id
  - snippet
  - score
  - metadata

6. Suggested project structure

- cmd/
  - server/ (main binary)
- internal/
  - api/ (HTTP handlers and routes)
  - service/ (orchestrator and business logic)
  - storage/ (database and file store abstractions)
  - processor/ (text extraction, segmentation, indexing)
  - model/ (data models)
  - queue/ (job queue adapters)
  - index/ (search index implementation)
  - config/ (settings and feature flags)
  - test/ (helper test utilities)
- pkg/
  - common/ (shared utilities)
- migrations/ (DB schema migrations)
- scripts/ (dev utilities, seed data)
- docs/ (design docs, runbooks)

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size): reject at upload with 400 and descriptive message; do not enqueue job.
- Processing errors: capture error details; mark document as failed; escalation to retry policy.
- Retries: implement idempotent retry for each step with exponential backoff; cap max retries; use dead-lettering for persistent failures.
- Transient vs permanent failures: transient (network, temporary parsing) retried; permanent (unsupported format) marked failed with guidance.
- Duplicate prevention: compute content hash during upload; check repository for existing document with same hash. If found, return existing document_id and status; do not create new records or reprocess unless explicitly requested by the user (e.g., force reprocess flag).
- Idempotency: operations on document_id are idempotent; repeated API calls for the same hash return the same document reference.
- Observability: log errors with context; emit metrics for failure rates, retry counts, and processing durations.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests
  - Validation logic for file types and sizes
  - Hash computation and deduplication checks
  - Text extraction mocks for PDF/TMT/Markdown paths
  - Chunking/segmentation correctness (imaginary boundary tests)
  - Indexing data integrity (inverted index entries per chunk)

- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload, storage, extraction, segmentation, indexing, and search
  - Asynchronous processing flow with mock queue to verify state transitions (queued -> processing -> ready)
  - Retry mechanism by simulating transient failures at steps

- Performance tests
  - Large documents to test extraction and chunking latency
  - Concurrent uploads to test queue resilience and DB transaction handling

- Reliability tests
  - Failure scenarios: force a step to fail and ensure retry cycle completes or moves to dead-letter

- Security tests
  - Validate input handling to prevent injection, ensure file access isolation

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous vs synchronous processing: async improves responsiveness and throughput for large files but adds complexity; chosen to allow scalable, non-blocking processing with clear status reporting.
- Duplicate deduplication by hash: avoids waste but requires reliable hashing and content normalization; extremely large files require streaming hash to avoid loading whole file in memory.
- Indexing strategy: inverted index provides fast keyword search; adding embeddings improves semantic search but increases complexity; design allows incremental adoption of embeddings later.
- Extensibility: centralized configuration and a pluggable processor pipeline enable adding new formats with minimal changes; avoid tight coupling to specific file parsers.
- Data model simplicity: keeping a document-centric model with chunks ensures clean separation between storage, text, and index; potential future shift to document-collection or per-section indexing remains feasible.

Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / Frontend Interface | Authenticate, validate, route; provide status/search | Client HTTP requests | Responses with status, IDs, and search results |
| File Validator & Metadata Extractor | Validate type/size; compute hash; metadata | Uploaded file | Validation result, metadata, hash |
| Storage Service | Persist original file and metadata | File + metadata | Stored file references |
| Text Extraction Engine | Extract plain text from file | Stored file | Extracted text |
| Document Segmentation Service | Split text into chunks | Plain text | Chunks with metadata |
| Indexing Service | Build/update search index | Chunks + metadata | Index entries / readiness |
| Search Service | Execute queries; return results | Query + filters | Ranked results with snippets |
| Processing Orchestrator / Job Queue | Orchestrate steps; retry policy | Upload events | Updated processing state |
| Status & Notification Manager | Report status to API | Status updates | Status payloads |
| Deduplication & Repository Layer | Detect duplicates by hash | Hash + records | Reference to existing or new doc |
| Configuration & Extension Layer | Manage formats, pipelines, retries | Config data | Applied config |
| Logging & Observability | Telemetry collection | System events | Metrics/logs |

API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /documents | Submit a file for processing | Multipart/form-data with file; optional metadata | { document_id, hash, status } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Get processing status and metadata | document_id in path | { document_id, status, progress, last_error, metadata } |
| GET | /search | Search processed documents | query, filters, pagination | { results: [{document_id, score, snippet, metadata}], total } |
| GET | /health | Health check | none | { status: ok/ degraded } |
| (Admin) POST | /documents/{id}/retry | Trigger retry for failed document | document_id | { document_id, status, next_retry } |

Design tradeoff note: The design favors maintainability and clarity for a small team, with explicit separation of concerns, modular components, and an asynchronous processing path that can be extended to new formats and indexing methods without large rewrites. If semantic search becomes a priority, embeddings can be introduced in the Indexing Service with minimal surface area changes.

1. System overview
Design a backend for a document-processing and search service supporting PDFs, TXT, and Markdown. Users upload files; the system validates type/size, stores original and metadata, extracts text, splits text into searchable sections, builds a searchable index, and enables search over processed documents. Processing can be asynchronous with retry on failure. Reuploads of the same file should be deduplicated. The architecture is modular to allow easy addition of new formats, remains maintainable for a small team, and emphasizes clear data flow, error handling, and testability.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / REST API | Authenticate (if needed), receive uploads, expose search and status endpoints | HTTP requests with file and parameters | HTTP responses, events to queue |
| Ingestion Service | Validate file type/size, compute hash for dedup, store original and metadata, enqueue processing | Upload payload, metadata | Acknowledgement, processing task id |
| Storage Layer | Persist original files, metadata, and index data | File bytes, metadata | File references, metadata records |
| Text Extraction Service | Extracts raw text from documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown; extensible) | Original file reference | Document text payload, per-file metadata |
| Document Splitter / Sectionizer | Breaks text into searchable sections (logical blocks) | Full document text | List of sections with IDs, metadata |
| Indexer / Search Index | Creates and updates a full-text index over sections, supports searchable queries | Document sections | Inverted index entries, index shard data |
| Processing Orchestrator (Queue/Worker) | Orchestrates asynchronous tasks, retries failed steps, monitors progress | Processing tasks | Status updates, results | 
| Status & Metadata Store | Tracks processing status for each uploaded file (pending, processing, done, failed) | Processing events | Status views, history |
| Search Service | Accepts query, returns relevant documents/sections with snippets | Query text | Ranked results with document IDs and snippets |
| Deduplication & ID Resolver | Detects identical uploads via content hash to avoid duplicates | File hash, metadata | Existing document reference or new document entry |
| Observability & Monitoring | Logging, metrics, tracing, alerting | System events | Telemetry data, dashboards |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload: Client uploads a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with optional metadata (title, author). API validates size/type and computes a content hash. If hash matches an existing document, return existing document reference (no duplicates). Otherwise, store original file and metadata, create a processing task, and respond with task/document ID.
- Ingestion: Ingestion Service finalizes validation, stores file references, and enqueues a processing workflow (text extraction -> sectioning -> indexing).
- Text extraction: Extraction service reads the stored file, extracts plain text. On success, persists extracted text and marks step complete; on failure, raises a retriable error.
- Sectioning: Splitter converts text into logical sections (e.g., paragraphs, headings, or semantic blocks) with section IDs and positions. Persist sections tied to the document.
- Indexing: Indexer builds or updates a full-text index from sections. Create per-document index metadata to support efficient search and ranking.
- Status update: Processing orchestrator records status as processing, success, or failed. If a step fails, it is retried according to a policy (exponential backoff) up to a max attempts. On success of all steps, status becomes DONE and the document is searchable.
- Search: User submits a query via the Search API. The Search Service uses the index to retrieve top results, returning document IDs, section excerpts, and relevance scores. Clients may request pagination or snippets.
- Re-upload handling: If the same file is uploaded again (same hash), the system returns the existing document reference without creating duplicates and without reprocessing unless triggered by explicit override.
- Extensibility: New formats are supported by adding a new extractor module and a small metadata adapter; the core workflow remains unchanged.

4. REST API design

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /upload | Upload a document; starts ingestion | multipart/form-data: file, optional metadata (title, author, tags) | { document_id, status: "QUEUED"/"ACCEPTED" } or 409 if duplicate |
| GET | /documents/{id}/status | Get processing status and progress | - | { document_id, status, progress, steps_completed, errors? } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata and access info | - | { document_id, title, author, hash, size, created_at, status, index_id } |
| GET | /search | Search the processed documents | query=<text>&limit&offset | { results: [{ document_id, score, snippet, context}, ...], total } |
| GET | /documents/{id}/contents | Retrieve extracted text or sections (admin/preview) | - | { text, sections: [{ id, snippet, offset }], ... } |
| POST | /admin/reprocess | Trigger reprocessing of a document (manual retry) | { document_id } | { status, steps } |

5. Data model

- Document
  - id (UUID)
  - hash (SHA-256)
  - original_filename
  - size_bytes
  - mime_type
  - metadata: { title, author, tags }
  - created_at
  - updated_at
  - status: { PENDING, PROCESSING, DONE, FAILED }
  - index_id (reference to index)

- DocumentText
  - document_id
  - text
  - extracted_at

- Section
  - id (per-document)
  - document_id
  - section_index
  - text
  - start_offset
  - end_offset
  - metadata (e.g., heading level, type)

- Index
  - id
  - document_id
  - index_data (structured in underlying search engine)
  - created_at
  - updated_at

- ProcessingTask
  - id
  - document_id
  - step (TEXT_EXTRACTION, SECTIONING, INDEXING)
  - status: { PENDING, RUNNING, SUCCESS, FAILED }
  - attempts
  - last_updated

- AuditLog
  - id
  - document_id
  - event
  - timestamp
  - details

6. Suggested project structure

- cmd/
  - api-server/main.go (REST API server)
- internal/
  - api/
    - handlers.go
    - request_types.go
  - service/
    - ingestion.go
    - extraction.go
    - sectioning.go
    - indexing.go
    - orchestrator.go
    - search.go
  - model/
    - document.go
    - section.go
    - index.go
    - task.go
  - storage/
    - storage.go (abstracted interfaces for blob/file store and relational metadata DB)
    - blob_store.go
    - db.go
  - queue/
    - worker.go (background workers, retry policy)
  - extractor/
    - formats/
      - pdf_extractor.go
      - txt_extractor.go
      - md_extractor.go
  - splitter/
    - splitter.go
  - tests/
    - unit/
    - integration/
- pkg/
  - search/
    - index.go (abstracted interface over a search engine)
- config/
  - config.go
- docs/
  - design.md

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size) are returned as 400 with actionable messages.
- Transient failures in any step (IO errors, external services) raise a retriable error. Processing tasks implement exponential backoff with a max retry count (e.g., 5 attempts).
- Idempotent deduplication: compute content hash on upload. If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document_id with status DONE (or current status); do not reprocess unless explicitly requested (or hash indicates new version).
- Partial failures: if a step fails, the orchestrator stores which steps succeeded, enabling resumption from the failed step without redoing completed steps.
- Duplicate prevention: enforce unique constraint on (hash) at the Document level. For reuploads, skip creating a new Document; optionally attach a new metadata version as an updateable field.
- Observability: centralize error rates, retries, and processing times. Emit metrics for per-step success rates and queue backlogs.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests
  - Extractors for each format produce expected text from sample files.
  - Section splitter yields coherent blocks for representative documents.
  - Hashing/deduplication behavior for identical and differing files.
  - API handlers validate inputs and return correct status codes.
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end flow for a PDF/TXT/MD: upload -> ingestion -> extraction -> sectioning -> indexing -> search -> status checks.
  - Retry mechanism: simulate transient failures and verify backoff and eventual success.
  - Duplicate upload test: same file hash does not create duplicates; status remains consistent.
- Contract tests
  - Ensure API contracts with search engine interface are honored (documented schemas).
- Performance tests
  - Indexing and search on growing dataset; measure latency and relevancy.
- Security tests (if auth is added)
  - Validate access to documents and search results based on permissions.

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous vs synchronous: Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and resilience, but adds complexity in eventual consistency and debugging. A state-machine approach with clear status per document mitigates this.
- Deduplication by hash: Simple and effective for identical content, but may require handling of metadata/versioning for minor changes. If versioning is needed, consider a separate field to mark canonical document vs. variant.
- Extensibility: Abstract extractors and a plugin-like format registry enable new formats with minimal impact. However, runtime discovery and dependency management require discipline.
- Indexing strategy: A centralized index simplifies search but may become a bottleneck at scale. For small teams, a single-index or small shard approach with a pluggable backend (e.g., Elastic, MeiliSearch) keeps complexity low.
- Data model simplicity: Separate text and sections aids fine-grained search and snippet extraction but increases storage and coordination complexity. The design keeps per-document metadata light and stores sections for richer search facets.

Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / REST API | Accept uploads, expose search and status | HTTP requests | Acknowledgements, task IDs |
| Ingestion Service | Validate, dedupe, store, enqueue processing | File, metadata | Processing task initiation |
| Storage Layer | Persist files and metadata | File bytes, metadata | File references, records |
| Text Extraction Service | Extracts text from documents | File reference | Extracted text, extraction metadata |
| Document Splitter / Sectionizer | Create searchable sections | Document text | Sections list with IDs |
| Indexer / Search Index | Build and maintain search index | Sections | Inverted index data |
| Processing Orchestrator | Manage tasks, retries, progress | Tasks/events | Status updates, results |
| Status & Metadata Store | Track processing status | Processing events | Status views/history |
| Search Service | Execute queries, return results | Query text | Results with scores/snippets |
| Deduplication & ID Resolver | Ensure idempotent uploads | File hash | Existing/new document reference |
| Observability & Monitoring | Collect metrics/logs | System events | Telemetry |

API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /upload | Upload and enqueue document | multipart: file, metadata | { document_id, status } or 409 if duplicate |
| GET | /documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status | - | { document_id, status, progress } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve metadata | - | { document_id, hash, title, author, size, status } |
| GET | /search | Query processed documents | query, limit, offset | { results: [{ document_id, score, snippet }], total } |
| GET | /documents/{id}/contents | Retrieve extracted text/sections | - | { text, sections } |
| POST | /admin/reprocess | Retry processing for a document | { document_id } | { status, steps_updated } |

Design tradeoff note: The design favors maintainability and clarity for a small team, accepting some complexity in the asynchronous workflow. If user-facing immediacy becomes critical, a slim synchronous processing path for small documents could be added as an optimization.

System overview
A lightweight, maintainable backend designed for document processing and search. It supports asynchronous processing, retryable steps, deduplicated uploads, easy extension for new formats, and clear visibility into processing status. Core goals: correctness, reliability, extensibility, and small-team friendliness.

Main components and responsibilities
- Ingestion API
  - Validate file type and size, deduplicate uploads, persist metadata and original file references.
  - Emit processing task to the queue if accepted.
- Job/Worker Processor
  - Asynchronously handle processing steps per document: text extraction, section segmentation, indexing, and status updates.
  - Implement retry logic for transient failures with exponential backoff.
- Text Extraction Service
  - Convert PDFs, TXT, Markdown to plain text; pluggable to support new formats.
- Document Divider
  - Split text into searchable sections (paragraphs, headings, or normalized chunks).
- Index Service
  - Build and maintain a searchable index (e.g., inverted index or lightweight vector index for semantic search).
- Metadata and Storage
  - Persist original file, extracted text, sections, and index metadata; store in a durable store.
- Search API
  - Accept queries, return relevant documents/sections with scores.
- Deduplication & Idempotency
  - Ensure repeated uploads of the same file do not create duplicates (checksum-based dedupe).
- Status Tracking
  - Persist per-file processing status, progress, and results; provide status API.
- Configuration & Plugins
  - Abstract format handlers and processing steps to ease adding new formats.

End-to-end processing workflow
- Upload
  - Client uploads a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with metadata (optional title, user id, etc.).
  - Ingestion API validates MIME type, size, computes a content hash, checks for existing record by hash.
  - If new: store original file blob and metadata; create a Document entity with status “QUEUED”; enqueue a processing job.
  - If duplicate: return existing document identifier with status.
- Processing pipeline (asynchronous)
  - Stage 1: Text Extraction → extracted_text stored; status updated to “TEXT_EXTRACTED”.
  - Stage 2: Section Division → sections stored; status updated to “SECTIONS_READY”.
  - Stage 3: Indexing → index entries created; status updated to “INDEXED”.
  - Stage 4: Complete → final status “READY” with searchable index reference.
- Search
  - Client queries the search API; backend executes against the index, returns hits with document references and snippets.
- Retry & failure
  - If any stage fails, job is retried with backoff; after max retries, status becomes “FAILED” and a reason is recorded.
- Reuse
  - Repeated uploads use hash-based deduplication; existing metadata and index are reused unless a reprocess is explicitly requested.

REST API design
- Endpoints focus on clarity, idempotency, and progressive status reporting.

Data model (high level)
- Document: id, user_id, filename, mime_type, size, hash, storage_uri, status, created_at, updated_at, error_message
- TextContent: document_id, text, extracted_at
- Section: document_id, index, section_id, text, range_start, range_end
- IndexRecord: document_id, index_type (inverted, vector), metadata, created_at
- ProcessingJob: id, document_id, stage, status, retry_count, last_attempt_at, next_attempt_at, error_message
- AuditLog: document_id, event, timestamp, details

Data flow
- Ingestion → Document (QUEUED) → ProcessingJob (TEXT_EXTRACTION) → TextContent → Section → IndexRecord → Document (READY)

Code organization (suggested)
- cmd/
  - api_server.go (HTTP router, wiring)
- internal/
  - handlers/
    - ingestion_handler.go
    - search_handler.go
  - services/
    - file_storage.go
    - text_extractor.go
    - sectionizer.go
    - indexer.go
    - search_engine.go
  - models/
    - document.go
    - text_content.go
    - section.go
    - index_record.go
    - processing_job.go
  - queue/
    - job_queue.go (abstract interface; concrete impl using a message broker)
  - repo/
    - document_repo.go
    - index_repo.go
    - storage_repo.go
  - config/
    - config.go
  - util/
    - hash.go
    - validation.go
- pkg/
  - codecs/ (parsers for PDF/TXT/Markdown)
  - metrics/ (basic instrumentation)
- tests/
  - unit/ and integration/

Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
- Validation
  - Ingest: accept only supported mime types and size limits; reject otherwise with 400.
- Idempotency key
  - Compute content hash (e.g., SHA-256) on upload; if a document with same hash exists, return existing id and status (idempotent behavior).
- Retries
  - ProcessingJob has retry_count with exponential backoff; transient errors (IO, network) retried automatically.
- Failure handling
  - If a step permanently fails (e.g., unsupported content within a file, irrecoverable parsing error), mark as FAILED with reason; alert hooks can notify users if configured.
- Duplicate protection
  - Hash-based dedupe ensures no duplicates for identical content; for partial reuploads, treat as updates if metadata differs (optional).
- Consistency
  - Use transactional operations for interdependent steps where possible (e.g., store text before indexing).

Testing plan
- Unit tests
  - Validate validators, hash-based dedupe, and small components (text extraction stubs, sectionizer).
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end flow with a temporary in-memory or local storage backend; simulate upload, processing through each stage, and search responses.
- Fault injection tests
  - Simulate failures in extraction, sectioning, and indexing to verify retry behavior and status updates.
- Performance tests
  - Basic load test on a modest dataset; ensure asynchronous processing and backpressure handling.
- Regression tests
  - Ensure adding a new format requires minimal changes (format plugin interface remains stable).
- Security tests
  - Validate file storage isolation per user; enforce size/type guards; ensure no arbitrary code execution via parsing.

Design tradeoffs
- Simplicity vs. scalability: Start with a single broker and local storage; can switch to scalable queue (e.g., RabbitMQ/Kafka) and distributed storage later.
- Text extraction fidelity: Use rule-based extractors with fallbacks; heavy NLP can be abstracted behind tools; trade-off between accuracy and speed.
- Index type: Inverted index for exact/near match vs. simple vector search. Start with inverted index for straightforward search, add vector search later.
- Duplication policy: Hash-based dedupe is robust for exact duplicates; differing metadata with identical content might still be treated as duplicates unless explicit update logic is added.
- Extensibility: Abstract format handlers via a plugin-like interface; keep concrete handlers isolated to reduce blast radius when adding formats.

Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ingestion API | Validate, dedupe, persist metadata, trigger processing | Upload request (file bytes, metadata) | Document created or existing document reference, status QUEUED |
| Job/Worker Processor | Orchestrate processing steps with retries | Processing jobs from queue | Updated document state, stored text, sections, index |
| Text Extraction Service | Extract text from PDFs, TXT, Markdown; pluggable | Document, file blob | extracted_text, extraction metadata |
| Document Divider | Split text into searchable sections | Extracted text | Sections (with metadata) |
| Index Service | Build and maintain search index | Sections, documents | Index records, searchable index ready |
| Metadata and Storage | Persist files and metadata | Original file, metadata | Stored file reference, metadata records |
| Search API | Execute queries against index, return results | Query string(s) | Hits with relevance, snippets, document refs |
| Deduplication & Idempotency | Ensure no duplicate records for same content | File hash, metadata | Single document record, idempotent result |
| Status Tracking | Report per-file processing status | Document IDs, status requests | Status data (QUEUED, TEXT_EXTRACTED, INDEXED, READY, FAILED) |

API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /api/documents/upload | Upload and enqueue processing | file (binary), metadata (optional) | { document_id, status: QUEUED, hash } or existing document payload |
| GET | /api/documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status | document_id | { document_id, status, progress, error_message } |
| GET | /api/search | Search documents | query, optional filters, limit | [{ document_id, score, snippet }] |
| GET | /api/documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata | document_id | { id, metadata, stored_uri, created_at, status } |

Notes
- Pseudocode or interface sketches can be used for plugin points (text_extractor interface, sectionizer interface, indexer interface) to illustrate extensibility.

Overall, the design emphasizes maintainability, clear boundaries between ingestion, processing, and search, and the ability to add new document formats with minimal disruption.

Visible prior context:
[Proposal 1]
1. System overview
The service enables users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files, validates size and type, stores originals with metadata, extracts text, segments it into searchable sections, builds an index, and supports search across documents. Processing can be asynchronous, with retry on failures. Duplicate uploads of the same file (by content hash) are deduplicated. The design emphasizes modularity, easy extension for new formats, maintainability, and a small developer footprint.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / Frontend API | Accepts uploads, searches, and status queries; authenticates and routes to services. | HTTP requests (upload, search, status). | HTTP responses with results/ statuses. |
| Upload Validator | Verifies file type, size, and computes content hash for deduplication. | File, metadata. | Validation result, content hash, allowed/denied flag. |
| Storage Service | Persists original file and metadata; serves for retrieval. | File bytes, metadata. | Storage location reference (ID/URI). |
| Metadata Store | Stores document metadata (owner, upload time, hash, format, status). | Metadata records. | Metadata IDs, status updates. |
| Text Extraction Engine | Extracts raw text from supported formats. | Original file reference. | Extracted text payload. |
| Document Divider / Segmenter | Splits extracted text into searchable sections (e.g., by headings, paragraphs). | Extracted text. | List of text segments with metadata (offsets, tags). |
| Indexer / Search Engine | Builds and maintains a searchable index from segments; supports queries. | Text segments. | Inverted index entries; searchable index state. |
| Processing Orchestrator (Worker / Queue) | Orchestrates asynchronous processing steps; handles retries on failure; tracks progress. | Upload event or message with document reference. | Task progress/status updates; re-queue on retryable failures. |
| Status & Progress Reporter | Exposes processing status per file/document; reports completed/failed/in-progress. | Metadata/processing state. | Status payloads for API clients. |
| Deduplication & Registry | Detects repeated uploads (by content hash) and reuses existing records; avoids duplicates. | Content hash, existing records. | Reference to existing document or new document record. |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload request arrives via API Gateway.
- Validation:
  - Check MIME type and extension against supported list (PDF, TXT, MD/Markdown).
  - Check file size against configured maximum.
  - Compute content hash (e.g., SHA-256) to enable deduplication.
- Deduplication:
  - If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document reference and status; do not create new storage or index entries.
  - If new, create a document record with status “queued” and store the original file and basic metadata.
- Persistence:
  - Persist original file in file storage (e.g., object store) and save metadata in the metadata store.
- Asynchronous processing (orchestrated by a queue):
  - Enqueue a processing job for the new document.
  - Step 1: Text Extraction Engine extracts text from the file.
  - Step 2: Divider splits text into sections/segments.
  - Step 3: Indexer builds/upserts the index from segments.
  - Step 4: Update document status to “completed” with index reference.
- Status reporting:
  - Clients can poll or subscribe to status; statuses include: queued, extracting, dividing, indexing, completed, failed, retrying.
- Search:
  - Search requests query the inverted index; results map back to document segments and provide document metadata and snippet highlights.

4. REST API design

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /upload | Upload a file for processing | multipart/form-data: file, optional metadata (owner, tags) | { document_id, status: "queued" |, hash } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata and status | - | { id, owner, format, hash, status, created_at, updated_at, index_id } |
| POST | /search | Search across documents | { query, filters? (document_id, date range, tags) } | { results: [ { document_id, snippet, score, highlights, metadata } ] } |
| GET | /documents/{id}/status | Get processing status/details | - | { id, status, progress, steps: [{ name, status, detail }] } |
| GET | /health | System health | - | { status: "ok" } |

Notes:
- The /upload endpoint returns early with a document_id; the client can poll /documents/{id}/status or subscribe to progress.
- Search results include document_id, snippet, score, and highlights mapped to segments.

5. Data model

- Document
  - id (UUID)
  - owner_id (optional)
  - original_filename
  - hash (SHA-256)
  - format (PDF/TXT/MD)
  - size_bytes
  - created_at
  - updated_at
  - status (queued, extracting, dividing, indexing, completed, failed)
  - index_id (reference to index state)
  - metadata (JSON blob for arbitrary fields)

- Segment
  - id (UUID)
  - document_id (FK)
  - segment_index
  - text
  - start_offset, end_offset (character offsets)
  - metadata (section_heading, tags)

- IndexEntry (per term)
  - term
  - document_id
  - segment_id
  - score
  - metadata ( tf-idf-ish stats, positions )

- OriginalFileStorage
  - records mapping document_id → storage_uri, storage_bucket, version, checksum

- ProcessingJob
  - id (UUID)
  - document_id
  - step (extraction/division/indexing)
  - status (queued/in-progress/succeeded/failed/retrying)
  - tries
  - last_updated

6. Suggested project structure

- cmd/
  - api_server/ (HTTP API endpoints)
- internal/
  - api/
  - validation/
  - storage/
  - metadata/
  - text_extractor/
  - segmenter/
  - index/
  - queue/
  - processor/
  - reporting/
- pkg/
  - models/
  - errors/
- config/
  - default.yaml
- test/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- docs/
  - architecture.md

Code organization principles:
- Clear separation between API layer, domain logic, and infrastructure.
- Stateless HTTP API; use a small, durable storage for metadata and a separate object store for files.
- Dependency injection to swap out format handlers and storage backends.
- Single-responsibility components with well-defined interfaces.

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size) return 400 with actionable error codes.
- Transient failures (network/storage hiccups, temporary extractor errors) are retryable:
  - Processing jobs are placed in a durable queue with exponential backoff.
  - If a step fails with a retryable error, mark the job as retrying and re-queue; cap total retries (e.g., 5) with backoff.
- Non-retryable failures (corrupt file, unsupported content) mark document status as failed with reason.
- Duplicate prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload.
  - Before creating a new document record, check if a document with the same hash exists.
  - If exists and status is completed or indexing done, return reference to existing document; if exists but processing incomplete, return current status and avoid duplicate storage.
- Idempotency:
  - idempotent /upload using the same hash can be supported by clients; server-side dedupe guarantees no duplicates.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests:
  - Validation module: reject invalid types, large sizes; accept valid ones.
  - Hashing and deduplication: identical files map to same document id.
  - Text extraction mocks for each format; ensure correct payload handling.
  - Segmenter: correct segmentation rules across sample texts.
  - Indexer: verify inverted index entries and retrieval results.
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end for a small PDF/TXT/MD sample: upload -> status progression -> search results.
  - Retry flow: simulate transient failure in a step and ensure retry occurs and eventual success.
  - Duplicate upload path: same file uploaded twice yields single document and proper references.
- Performance/load tests:
  - Concurrent uploads and searches; verify queue backpressure and acceptable latency.
- Security tests:
  - Validate access control on endpoints; ensure no leakage of raw files without authorization.

9. Design tradeoff

- Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and resilience but adds eventual consistency; search results may depend on the index being built; mitigate with status indicators and optional synchronous previews if needed.
- Deduplication by content hash reduces storage and confusion but requires careful handling of hash collisions (negligible risk with SHA-256, but plan for collision-safe paths).
- Support for new formats is designed via a plug-in style architecture for text extraction and segmentation; initial formats (PDF, TXT, MD) implemented with minimal dependencies, with an extensible registry for future formats.
- Indexing strategy focuses on a simple inverted index with per-document segments; this balances search quality and simplicity for a small team, with room to upgrade to full-text search engines if scale grows.

9. Design tradeoffs

Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
- Validation
  - Ingest: accept only supported mime types and size limits; reject otherwise with 400.
- Idempotency key
  - Compute content hash (e.g., SHA-256) on upload; if a document with same hash exists, return existing id and status (idempotent behavior).
- Retries
  - ProcessingJob has retry_count with exponential backoff; transient errors (IO, network) retried automatically.
- Failure handling
  - If a step permanently fails (e.g., unsupported content within a file, irrecoverable parsing error), mark as FAILED with reason; alert hooks can notify users if configured.
- Duplicate protection
  - Hash-based dedupe ensures no duplicates for identical content; for partial reuploads, treat as updates if metadata differs (optional).
- Consistency
  - Use transactional operations for interdependent steps where possible (e.g., store text before indexing).

Testing plan

  - Basic load test on a modest dataset; ensure asynchronous processing and backpressure handling.
- Regression tests
  - Ensure adding a new format requires minimal changes (format plugin interface remains stable).
- Security tests
  - Validate file storage isolation per user; enforce size/type guards; ensure no arbitrary code execution via parsing.

Design tradeoffs
- Simplicity vs. scalability: Start with a single broker and local storage; can switch to scalable queue (e.g., RabbitMQ/Kafka) and distributed storage later.
- Text extraction fidelity: Use rule-based extractors with fallbacks; heavy NLP can be abstracted behind tools; trade-off between accuracy and speed.
- Index type: Inverted index for exact/near match vs. simple vector search. Start with inverted index for straightforward search, add vector search later.
- Duplication policy: Hash-based dedupe is robust for exact duplicates; differing metadata with identical content might still be treated as duplicates unless explicit update logic is added.
- Extensibility: Abstract format handlers via a plugin-like interface; keep concrete handlers isolated to reduce blast radius when adding formats.

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload: Client uploads a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with optional metadata (title, author). API validates size/type and computes a content hash. If hash matches an existing document, return existing document reference (no duplicates). Otherwise, store original file and metadata, create a processing task, and respond with task/document ID.
- Ingestion: Ingestion Service finalizes validation, stores file references, and enqueues a processing workflow (text extraction -> sectioning -> indexing).
- Text extraction: Extraction service reads the stored file, extracts plain text. On success, persists extracted text and marks step complete; on failure, raises a retriable error.
- Sectioning: Splitter converts text into logical sections (e.g., paragraphs, headings, or semantic blocks) with section IDs and positions. Persist sections tied to the document.
- Indexing: Indexer builds or updates a full-text index from sections. Create per-document index metadata to support efficient search and ranking.
- Status update: Processing orchestrator records status as processing, success, or failed. If a step fails, it is retried according to a policy (exponential backoff) up to a max attempts. On success of all steps, status becomes DONE and the document is searchable.
- Search: User submits a query via the Search API. The Search Service uses the index to retrieve top results, returning document IDs, section excerpts, and relevance scores. Clients may request pagination or snippets.
- Re-upload handling: If the same file is uploaded again (same hash), the system returns the existing document reference without creating duplicates and without reprocessing unless triggered by explicit override.
- Extensibility: New formats are supported by adding a new extractor module and a small metadata adapter; the core workflow remains unchanged.

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size) are returned as 400 with actionable messages.
- Transient failures in any step (IO errors, external services) raise a retriable error. Processing tasks implement exponential backoff with a max retry count (e.g., 5 attempts).
- Idempotent deduplication: compute content hash on upload. If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document_id with status DONE (or current status); do not reprocess unless explicitly requested (or hash indicates new version).
- Partial failures: if a step fails, the orchestrator stores which steps succeeded, enabling resumption from the failed step without redoing completed steps.
- Duplicate prevention: enforce unique constraint on (hash) at the Document level. For reuploads, skip creating a new Document; optionally attach a new metadata version as an updateable field.
- Observability: centralize error rates, retries, and processing times. Emit metrics for per-step success rates and queue backlogs.

  - Hashing/deduplication behavior for identical and differing files.
  - API handlers validate inputs and return correct status codes.
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end flow for a PDF/TXT/MD: upload -> ingestion -> extraction -> sectioning -> indexing -> search -> status checks.
  - Retry mechanism: simulate transient failures and verify backoff and eventual success.
  - Duplicate upload test: same file hash does not create duplicates; status remains consistent.
- Contract tests

- Performance tests

- Security tests (if auth is added)
  - Validate access to documents and search results based on permissions.

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous vs synchronous: Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and resilience, but adds complexity in eventual consistency and debugging. A state-machine approach with clear status per document mitigates this.
- Deduplication by hash: Simple and effective for identical content, but may require handling of metadata/versioning for minor changes. If versioning is needed, consider a separate field to mark canonical document vs. variant.
- Extensibility: Abstract extractors and a plugin-like format registry enable new formats with minimal impact. However, runtime discovery and dependency management require discipline.
- Indexing strategy: A centralized index simplifies search but may become a bottleneck at scale. For small teams, a single-index or small shard approach with a pluggable backend (e.g., Elastic, MeiliSearch) keeps complexity low.
- Data model simplicity: Separate text and sections aids fine-grained search and snippet extraction but increases storage and coordination complexity. The design keeps per-document metadata light and stores sections for richer search facets.

System overview
A lightweight, maintainable backend designed for document processing and search. It supports asynchronous processing, retryable steps, deduplicated uploads, easy extension for new formats, and clear visibility into processing status. Core goals: correctness, reliability, extensibility, and small-team friendliness.

Main components and responsibilities
- Ingestion API

- Job/Worker Processor
  - Asynchronously handle processing steps per document: text extraction, section segmentation, indexing, and status updates.

- Document Divider
  - Split text into searchable sections (paragraphs, headings, or normalized chunks).
- Index Service
  - Build and maintain a searchable index (e.g., inverted index or lightweight vector index for semantic search).
- Metadata and Storage
  - Persist original file, extracted text, sections, and index metadata; store in a durable store.
- Search API
  - Accept queries, return relevant documents/sections with scores.
- Deduplication & Idempotency
  - Ensure repeated uploads of the same file do not create duplicates (checksum-based dedupe).
- Status Tracking
  - Persist per-file processing status, progress, and results; provide status API.
- Configuration & Plugins
  - Abstract format handlers and processing steps to ease adding new formats.

End-to-end processing workflow
- Upload
  - Client uploads a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with metadata (optional title, user id, etc.).
  - Ingestion API validates MIME type, size, computes a content hash, checks for existing record by hash.

- Processing pipeline (asynchronous)
  - Stage 1: Text Extraction → extracted_text stored; status updated to “TEXT_EXTRACTED”.
  - Stage 2: Section Division → sections stored; status updated to “SECTIONS_READY”.
  - Stage 3: Indexing → index entries created; status updated to “INDEXED”.
  - Stage 4: Complete → final status “READY” with searchable index reference.
- Search
  - Client queries the search API; backend executes against the index, returns hits with document references and snippets.
- Retry & failure
  - If any stage fails, job is retried with backoff; after max retries, status becomes “FAILED” and a reason is recorded.
- Reuse
  - Repeated uploads use hash-based deduplication; existing metadata and index are reused unless a reprocess is explicitly requested.

REST API design
- Endpoints focus on clarity, idempotency, and progressive status reporting.

- Document: id, user_id, filename, mime_type, size, hash, storage_uri, status, created_at, updated_at, error_message

Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
- Validation

- Idempotency key
  - Compute content hash (e.g., SHA-256) on upload; if a document with same hash exists, return existing id and status (idempotent behavior).
- Retries
  - ProcessingJob has retry_count with exponential backoff; transient errors (IO, network) retried automatically.
- Failure handling
  - If a step permanently fails (e.g., unsupported content within a file, irrecoverable parsing error), mark as FAILED with reason; alert hooks can notify users if configured.
- Duplicate protection
  - Hash-based dedupe ensures no duplicates for identical content; for partial reuploads, treat as updates if metadata differs (optional).
- Consistency
  - Use transactional operations for interdependent steps where possible (e.g., store text before indexing).

Testing plan
- Unit tests

Design tradeoffs
- Simplicity vs. scalability: Start with a single broker and local storage; can switch to scalable queue (e.g., RabbitMQ/Kafka) and distributed storage later.
- Text extraction fidelity: Use rule-based extractors with fallbacks; heavy NLP can be abstracted behind tools; trade-off between accuracy and speed.
- Index type: Inverted index for exact/near match vs. simple vector search. Start with inverted index for straightforward search, add vector search later.
- Duplication policy: Hash-based dedupe is robust for exact duplicates; differing metadata with identical content might still be treated as duplicates unless explicit update logic is added.
- Extensibility: Abstract format handlers via a plugin-like interface; keep concrete handlers isolated to reduce blast radius when adding formats.

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size): reject at upload with 400 and descriptive message; do not enqueue job.

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size) are returned as 400 with actionable messages.
- Transient failures in any step (IO errors, external services) raise a retriable error. Processing tasks implement exponential backoff with a max retry count (e.g., 5 attempts).

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous vs synchronous: Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and resilience, but adds complexity in eventual consistency and debugging. A state-machine approach with clear status per document mitigates this.
- Deduplication by hash: Simple and effective for identical content, but may require handling of metadata/versioning for minor changes. If versioning is needed, consider a separate field to mark canonical document vs. variant.
- Extensibility: Abstract extractors and a plugin-like format registry enable new formats with minimal impact. However, runtime discovery and dependency management require discipline.
- Indexing strategy: A centralized index simplifies search but may become a bottleneck at scale. For small teams, a single-index or small shard approach with a pluggable backend (e.g., Elastic, MeiliSearch) keeps complexity low.
- Data model simplicity: Separate text and sections aids fine-grained search and snippet extraction but increases storage and coordination complexity. The design keeps per-document metadata light and stores sections for richer search facets.

  - If a step permanently fails (e.g., unsupported content within a file, irrecoverable parsing error), mark as FAILED with reason; alert hooks can notify users if configured.
- Duplicate protection
  - Hash-based dedupe ensures no duplicates for identical content; for partial reuploads, treat as updates if metadata differs (optional).
- Consistency
  - Use transactional operations for interdependent steps where possible (e.g., store text before indexing).

Testing plan
- Unit tests
  - Validate validators, hash-based dedupe, and small components (text extraction stubs, sectionizer).

  - End-to-end flow with a temporary in-memory or local storage backend; simulate upload, processing through each stage, and search responses.

  - Simulate failures in extraction, sectioning, and indexing to verify retry behavior and status updates.

  - Ensure adding a new format requires minimal changes (format plugin interface remains stable).
- Security tests
  - Validate file storage isolation per user; enforce size/type guards; ensure no arbitrary code execution via parsing.

Design tradeoffs
- Simplicity vs. scalability: Start with a single broker and local storage; can switch to scalable queue (e.g., RabbitMQ/Kafka) and distributed storage later.
- Text extraction fidelity: Use rule-based extractors with fallbacks; heavy NLP can be abstracted behind tools; trade-off between accuracy and speed.
- Index type: Inverted index for exact/near match vs. simple vector search. Start with inverted index for straightforward search, add vector search later.
- Duplication policy: Hash-based dedupe is robust for exact duplicates; differing metadata with identical content might still be treated as duplicates unless explicit update logic is added.
- Extensibility: Abstract format handlers via a plugin-like interface; keep concrete handlers isolated to reduce blast radius when adding formats.

- Ingestion: Ingestion Service finalizes validation, stores file references, and enqueues a processing workflow (text extraction -> sectioning -> indexing).
- Text extraction: Extraction service reads the stored file, extracts plain text. On success, persists extracted text and marks step complete; on failure, raises a retriable error.
- Sectioning: Splitter converts text into logical sections (e.g., paragraphs, headings, or semantic blocks) with section IDs and positions. Persist sections tied to the document.
- Indexing: Indexer builds or updates a full-text index from sections. Create per-document index metadata to support efficient search and ranking.
- Status update: Processing orchestrator records status as processing, success, or failed. If a step fails, it is retried according to a policy (exponential backoff) up to a max attempts. On success of all steps, status becomes DONE and the document is searchable.
- Search: User submits a query via the Search API. The Search Service uses the index to retrieve top results, returning document IDs, section excerpts, and relevance scores. Clients may request pagination or snippets.
- Re-upload handling: If the same file is uploaded again (same hash), the system returns the existing document reference without creating duplicates and without reprocessing unless triggered by explicit override.
- Extensibility: New formats are supported by adding a new extractor module and a small metadata adapter; the core workflow remains unchanged.

4. REST API design

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /upload | Upload a document; starts ingestion | multipart/form-data: file, optional metadata (title, author, tags) | { document_id, status: "QUEUED"/"ACCEPTED" } or 409 if duplicate |
| GET | /documents/{id}/status | Get processing status and progress | - | { document_id, status, progress, steps_completed, errors? } |
| GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata and access info | - | { document_id, title, author, hash, size, created_at, status, index_id } |
| GET | /search | Search the processed documents | query=<text>&limit&offset | { results: [{ document_id, score, snippet, context}, ...], total } |
| GET | /documents/{id}/contents | Retrieve extracted text or sections (admin/preview) | - | { text, sections: [{ id, snippet, offset }], ... } |
| POST | /admin/reprocess | Trigger reprocessing of a document (manual retry) | { document_id } | { status, steps } |

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (invalid type/size) are returned as 400 with actionable messages.
- Transient failures in any step (IO errors, external services) raise a retriable error. Processing tasks implement exponential backoff with a max retry count (e.g., 5 attempts).
- Idempotent deduplication: compute content hash on upload. If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document_id with status DONE (or current status); do not reprocess unless explicitly requested (or hash indicates new version).
- Partial failures: if a step fails, the orchestrator stores which steps succeeded, enabling resumption from the failed step without redoing completed steps.
- Duplicate prevention: enforce unique constraint on (hash) at the Document level. For reuploads, skip creating a new Document; optionally attach a new metadata version as an updateable field.
- Observability: centralize error rates, retries, and processing times. Emit metrics for per-step success rates and queue backlogs.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests
  - Extractors for each format produce expected text from sample files.
  - Section splitter yields coherent blocks for representative documents.
  - Hashing/deduplication behavior for identical and differing files.
  - API handlers validate inputs and return correct status codes.
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end flow for a PDF/TXT/MD: upload -> ingestion -> extraction -> sectioning -> indexing -> search -> status checks.
  - Retry mechanism: simulate transient failures and verify backoff and eventual success.
  - Duplicate upload test: same file hash does not create duplicates; status remains consistent.
- Contract tests
  - Ensure API contracts with search engine interface are honored (documented schemas).
- Performance tests
  - Indexing and search on growing dataset; measure latency and relevancy.
- Security tests (if auth is added)
  - Validate access to documents and search results based on permissions.

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous vs synchronous: Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and resilience, but adds complexity in eventual consistency and debugging. A state-machine approach with clear status per document mitigates this.
- Deduplication by hash: Simple and effective for identical content, but may require handling of metadata/versioning for minor changes. If versioning is needed, consider a separate field to mark canonical document vs. variant.
- Extensibility: Abstract extractors and a plugin-like format registry enable new formats with minimal impact. However, runtime discovery and dependency management require discipline.
- Indexing strategy: A centralized index simplifies search but may become a bottleneck at scale. For small teams, a single-index or small shard approach with a pluggable backend (e.g., Elastic, MeiliSearch) keeps complexity low.
- Data model simplicity: Separate text and sections aids fine-grained search and snippet extraction but increases storage and coordination complexity. The design keeps per-document metadata light and stores sections for richer search facets.

API Table

| GET | /documents/{id}/contents | Retrieve extracted text/sections | - | { text, sections } |
| POST | /admin/reprocess | Retry processing for a document | { document_id } | { status, steps_updated } |

Design tradeoff note: The design favors maintainability and clarity for a small team, accepting some complexity in the asynchronous workflow. If user-facing immediacy becomes critical, a slim synchronous processing path for small documents could be added as an optimization.

[Proposal 4]
System overview
A lightweight, maintainable backend designed for document processing and search. It supports asynchronous processing, retryable steps, deduplicated uploads, easy extension for new formats, and clear visibility into processing status. Core goals: correctness, reliability, extensibility, and small-team friendliness.

Main components and responsibilities
- Ingestion API
  - Validate file type and size, deduplicate uploads, persist metadata and original file references.
  - Emit processing task to the queue if accepted.
- Job/Worker Processor
  - Asynchronously handle processing steps per document: text extraction, section segmentation, indexing, and status updates.
  - Implement retry logic for transient failures with exponential backoff.
- Text Extraction Service
  - Convert PDFs, TXT, Markdown to plain text; pluggable to support new formats.
- Document Divider
  - Split text into searchable sections (paragraphs, headings, or normalized chunks).
- Index Service
  - Build and maintain a searchable index (e.g., inverted index or lightweight vector index for semantic search).
- Metadata and Storage
  - Persist original file, extracted text, sections, and index metadata; store in a durable store.
- Search API
  - Accept queries, return relevant documents/sections with scores.
- Deduplication & Idempotency
  - Ensure repeated uploads of the same file do not create duplicates (checksum-based dedupe).
- Status Tracking
  - Persist per-file processing status, progress, and results; provide status API.
- Configuration & Plugins
  - Abstract format handlers and processing steps to ease adding new formats.

End-to-end processing workflow
- Upload
  - Client uploads a file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with metadata (optional title, user id, etc.).
  - Ingestion API validates MIME type, size, computes a content hash, checks for existing record by hash.
  - If new: store original file blob and metadata; create a Document entity with status “QUEUED”; enqueue a processing job.
  - If duplicate: return existing document identifier with status.
- Processing pipeline (asynchronous)
  - Stage 1: Text Extraction → extracted_text stored; status updated to “TEXT_EXTRACTED”.
  - Stage 2: Section Division → sections stored; status updated to “SECTIONS_READY”.
  - Stage 3: Indexing → index entries created; status updated to “INDEXED”.
  - Stage 4: Complete → final status “READY” with searchable index reference.
- Search
  - Client queries the search API; backend executes against the index, returns hits with document references and snippets.
- Retry & failure
  - If any stage fails, job is retried with backoff; after max retries, status becomes “FAILED” and a reason is recorded.
- Reuse
  - Repeated uploads use hash-based deduplication; existing metadata and index are reused unless a reprocess is explicitly requested.

REST API design
- Endpoints focus on clarity, idempotency, and progressive status reporting.

Data model (high level)
- Document: id, user_id, filename, mime_type, size, hash, storage_uri, status, created_at, updated_at, error_message
- TextContent: document_id, text, extracted_at
- Section: document_id, index, section_id, text, range_start, range_end
- IndexRecord: document_id, index_type (inverted, vector), metadata, created_at
- ProcessingJob: id, document_id, stage, status, retry_count, last_attempt_at, next_attempt_at, error_message
- AuditLog: document_id, event, timestamp, details

Data flow
- Ingestion → Document (QUEUED) → ProcessingJob (TEXT_EXTRACTION) → TextContent → Section → IndexRecord → Document (READY)

Code organization (suggested)
- cmd/
  - api_server.go (HTTP router, wiring)
- internal/
  - handlers/

Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
- Validation
  - Ingest: accept only supported mime types and size limits; reject otherwise with 400.
- Idempotency key
  - Compute content hash (e.g., SHA-256) on upload; if a document with same hash exists, return existing id and status (idempotent behavior).
- Retries
  - ProcessingJob has retry_count with exponential backoff; transient errors (IO, network) retried automatically.
- Failure handling
  - If a step permanently fails (e.g., unsupported content within a file, irrecoverable parsing error), mark as FAILED with reason; alert hooks can notify users if configured.
- Duplicate protection
  - Hash-based dedupe ensures no duplicates for identical content; for partial reuploads, treat as updates if metadata differs (optional).
- Consistency
  - Use transactional operations for interdependent steps where possible (e.g., store text before indexing).

Testing plan
- Unit tests
  - Validate validators, hash-based dedupe, and small components (text extraction stubs, sectionizer).
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end flow with a temporary in-memory or local storage backend; simulate upload, processing through each stage, and search responses.
- Fault injection tests

- Performance tests

- Regression tests
